chapter_title.ar: بَابُ الْقَوْلِ عِنْدَ الْإِصْبَاحِ وَ الْإِمْسَاءِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 48 – The words (to be spoken) during the morning and the evening

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/255)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن كالصحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (12/255)
text: يَقُولُ بَعْدَ الصُّبْحِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ رَبِّ الصَّبَاحِ الْحَمْدُ لِلَّهِ فَالِقِ الْإِصْبَاحِ ثَلَاثَ مَرَّاتٍ اللَّهُمَّ افْتَحْ لِي بَابَ الْأَمْرِ الَّذِي فِيهِ الْيُسْرُ وَ الْعَافِيَةُ اللَّهُمَّ هَيِّئْ لِي سَبِيلَهُ وَ بَصِّرْنِي مَخْرَجَهُ اللَّهُمَّ إِنْ كُنْتَ قَضَيْتَ لِأَحَدٍ مِنْ خَلْقِكَ عَلَيَّ مَقْدُرَةً بِالشَّرِّ فَخُذْهُ مِنْ بَيْنِ يَدَيْهِ وَ مِنْ خَلْفِهِ وَ عَنْ يَمِينِهِ وَ عَنْ شِمَالِهِ وَ مِنْ تَحْتِ قَدَمَيْهِ وَ مِنْ فَوْقِ رَأْسِهِ وَ اكْفِنِيهِ بِمَا شِئْتَ وَ مِنْ حَيْثُ شِئْتَ وَ كَيْفَ شِئْتَ .

en.hubeali: Ali, from his father, from Hammad, from Hareyz, from Zurara,

(It has been narrated) from Abu Ja’far<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘(You) should be saying after the morning, ‘The Praise is for Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, Lord<sup>azwj</sup> of the morning. The Praise is for Allah<sup>azwj</sup>, the Splitter of the morning’, three times. ‘O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! Open up for me the door of the Command which wherein is the prosperity and the good health. O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! Prepare its way for me and show me its exit. O Allah<sup>azwj</sup>! If You<sup>azwj</sup> have Ordained for anyone from Your<sup>azwj</sup> creatures capability upon me with the evil, so Seize him from his front, and his back, and from his right, and from his left, and from beneath his feet, and from above his head, and Enshroud him with whatever You<sup>azwj</sup> so Desire to, from wherever You<sup>azwj</sup> so Desire to, and however You<sup>azwj</sup> so Desire to’’.
en.sarwar: 18. Ali has narrated from his father from Hammad from Hariz from Zurara from abu Ja’far (a.s.) who has said the following: “One in the morning should say, ‘All praise belongs to Allah, the Lord of the morning, all praise belongs to Allah, who opens the morning, [ three times]. O Lord, open for me the door of the matter in which there is felicitation and good health, O Lord, prepare its way for me and show me the way out of it. O Lord, if You have decided that any of Your creatures should over power me in an evil way, seize him from all directions, front, back, right, left, above and below and suffice me by whatever means You like, whenever and however You want.’”
